selected_items.title: Yucatán limestone aquifer system and ocean infiltration
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack describes the subsurface hydrology of Yucatán as a geo-biological signal: porous limestone, underground rivers, cenotes, and ocean water infiltration from the Caribbean. This connects ancient Maya site selection to hidden water currents and earth-biology interfaces.
selected_items.source_claim: The Ocean is seeping beneath Yucatán through limestone. Yucatán is primarily limestone, highly porous, allowing caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes. Ocean water infiltrates through soil and travels through massive underground systems with flooded caverns and hidden currents from the Caribbean across the peninsula. Many areas remain unexplored. Maya saw cenotes as sacred gateways to the underworld connected to forces of nature.
selected_items.mechanism: Limestone porosity enables ocean-freshwater mixing and underground current systems that connect surface and subsurface hydrology across the peninsula.
selected_items.reader_change: Understand Yucatán's subsurface as a living water system where ocean and earth remain connected; recognize cenotes as markers of deep aquifer networks and potential biological significance of limestone-mediated water chemistry.
selected_items.source_support: The Ocean is already seeping beneath the land of Yucatán, and this phenomenon is growing stronger by the day... Yucatán is primarily formed of limestone, a highly porous type of rock that, over thousands of years, has allowed the creation of caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes... enormous flooded caverns and hidden currents that course through the subsurface practically from the Caribbean all the way to various points across the peninsula.
support_quotes: The Ocean is already seeping beneath the land of Yucatán, and this phenomenon is growing stronger by the day.
Yucatán is primarily formed of limestone, a highly porous type of rock that, over thousands of years, has allowed the creation of caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes.
enormous flooded caverns and hidden currents that course through the subsurface practically from the Caribbean all the way to various points across the peninsula.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain cenotes as natural sinkholes and water access points in limestone terrain; clarify how porous limestone allows ocean water to mix with freshwater aquifers; describe the biological relevance of mineral-rich underground water systems to Kruse's thesis on water chemistry and earth magnetism.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a geo observation without explicit treatment protocol. The value is in understanding earth-water systems as biological context, not as a direct medical intervention.

selected_items.title: EDS and internal water table collapse linked to charge accumulation
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack connects Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome pathophysiology to a specific biophysical mechanism: collapsed internal water table (160 to ~78) causing loss of insulation/grounding, leading to excess positive charge accumulation and loss of internal battery capacitance.
selected_items.source_claim: In EDS, the internal water table has collapsed from 160 to approximately 78, causing the patient to become uninsulated and ungrounded, accumulating excess positive charge while the internal battery lacks capacitance to absorb and ground the charge.
selected_items.mechanism: Collapsed internal water table → loss of insulation and grounding → accumulation of positive charge → insufficient capacitance in internal battery to absorb and ground charge.
selected_items.reader_change: Recognize EDS as a water-structure and charge-handling disorder, not only a collagen disorder; understand that grounding and water-table restoration may address the underlying charge dysregulation.
selected_items.source_support: Internal water table has likely collapsed (160 to ~ 78) = she is uninsulated / ungrounded so would accumulate way more + charge and internal batt lacks the capacitance to absorb and ground the charge.
support_quotes: Internal water table has likely collapsed (160 to ~ 78) = she is uninsulated / ungrounded so would accumulate way more + charge and internal batt lacks the capacitance to absorb and ground the charge.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain internal water table as the body's hydration and electrical conductivity baseline; clarify how water loss reduces insulation and grounding capacity; describe the internal battery as mitochondrial charge storage and how loss of capacitance leads to charge dysregulation in connective tissue.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a mechanistic claim about EDS pathophysiology. The writer should present it as Jack's biophysical model, not as proven biochemistry.
